codeReview.summary: The feature implements failure classification for MCP connections with two new types (McpFailureClass, McpConnectionError) in mcp.rs and retry-with-backoff logic in mcp_config.rs. Recoverable errors (timeout, connection refused) trigger retries with exponential backoff; non-recoverable errors (binary not found, protocol mismatch) fail immediately.

summary: PASS — VAL-MCP-003 satisfied. McpFailureClass correctly classifies connection failures: timeout/connection-refused → Recoverable (retry with backoff); binary-not-found/protocol-mismatch → NonRecoverable (immediate fail). McpConfigManager::connect_with_retry() implements the retry logic and marks server as Failed for non-recoverable errors. 23 unit tests cover the classification logic with comprehensive coverage of phase-specific error patterns.
